name: Softshell Crabs with Chive Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine flour and salt in a plastic bag. Add white pepper to taste.
2. Pat the crabs dry and add them to the flour mixture. Shake to coat evenly.
3. Heat butter in a large skillet until hot enough to hold all crabs in one layer.
4. Remove excess flour from the crabs by shaking them off.
5. Place the crabs in the hot butter and sauté over medium heat, turning occasionally, until golden brown and firm, about 3 to 5 minutes per side. Remove crabs to a baking sheet and keep warm at 300°F (150°C).
6. In the same skillet, add white wine and cook over high heat, scraping up any browned bits, until the wine is reduced by half, about 1 to 2 minutes.
7. Stir in whipping cream, Dijon mustard, and minced chives. Reduce heat to low and simmer until the mixture thickens slightly, about 2 to 3 more minutes.
8. Taste and adjust seasoning with salt and pepper if needed.
9. Serve by placing 2 crabs on each of 2 plates. Spoon half the sauce over each serving.
Makes 2 servings.
